title: Sabich

overview.description: A Middle Eastern pita filled with roasted aubergine, boiled eggs, hummus and fresh salad. Adapted for the Chapman Kitchen by making amba optional while keeping the spirit of the original.

method: Brush the aubergine slices with olive oil.
Roast at 200°C fan (220°C conventional) for 25–30 minutes, turning halfway, until soft and lightly browned.
Place the eggs in cold water and bring to the boil.
Cool under cold running water, peel and slice in half.
Mix together the tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, parsley and lemon juice.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Fill with roasted aubergine, salad and a boiled egg.

learning.did-you-know: Sabich is believed to have been introduced to Israel by Iraqi Jews and has become one of the country's best-loved street foods.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to roast aubergine, cook perfect hard-boiled eggs and balance warm and cold components in a sandwich.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Introduces another style of filled flatbread using familiar ingredients while encouraging homemade hummus and pita.
chapman-tips: Roasting the aubergine gives a cleaner flavour than frying and uses less oil.
Homemade hummus makes a noticeable difference.
Warm the pita just before assembling.
Amba is traditional but optional if it's difficult to find.
notes.batch-cooking: Roast the aubergine and boil the eggs ahead of time. Assemble just before serving.
notes.leftovers: The roasted aubergine is excellent in salads or wraps the following day.
